title: Cyclosorus × holttumianus
intro: Cyclosorus × holttumianus, also known as the Holttum's fern, is a hybrid species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus × holttumianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, and is also used in the form of tinctures and infusions to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus × holttumianus is a hybrid species of fern within the genus Cyclosorus. The genus Cyclosorus itself is part of the family Thelypteridaceae. Thelypteridaceae is a family of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or autumn ferns. The classification of Cyclosorus × holttumianus also recognizes the order Polypodiales, which is a large order of ferns. This classification places Cyclosorus × holttumianus within the kingdom Plantae.
